title: Water Fill Issue
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not filling with enough water within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error code i10 indicates the dishwasher detected insufficient water fill. The water level did not reach the required minimum within the allotted time. The float switch or turbidity sensor signals the control board that water has not entered or has entered too slowly.

causes.cause: Water supply valve not fully open
Kinked or clogged water inlet hose

symptoms: Dishwasher stops early in the cycle
Little or no water in the tub
Dishes come out dirty and dry

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the water supply valve under the kitchen sink. Ensure it is fully open. Turn it off and back on.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the water supply line from the valve to the dishwasher for kinks or damage.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet valve screen
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the inlet hose at the dishwasher. Remove and clean the small mesh filter screen in the inlet valve.

fixSteps.title: Test water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the inlet valve solenoid coil. It should read 500-1500 ohms. If open or shorted, replace the valve.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement or the float switch is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher i10 error water fill fix

faq.q: Can I reset the i10 code?
faq.a: Press and hold the cancel/drain button for 3 seconds, or turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. If the underlying issue is resolved, the code will clear.

faq.q: Where is the water supply valve?
faq.a: It is typically located under the kitchen sink, on the hot water pipe. It is a small valve with a handle or knob.

faq.q: Is i10 related to low water pressure?
faq.a: Yes. The dishwasher requires at least 20 PSI water pressure. Low pressure can cause slow filling and trigger i10.

title: Drain Issue
shortDescription: The dishwasher is unable to drain water properly.
fullDescription: Error code i20 indicates the dishwasher could not drain water within the expected time. The water level remains above the minimum threshold after the drain pump has been running. This usually indicates a physical blockage in the drain system.

causes.cause: Clogged drain filter or food trap
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed
High drain loop or air gap blocked

symptoms: Standing water in bottom of dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack. Twist and remove the drain filter assembly at the bottom of the tub. Clean off all food debris, grease, and buildup under running water.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out and inspect the drain hose for kinks. Disconnect the hose at the sink or disposal end and check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check garbage disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a garbage disposal, ensure the knockout plug was removed from the disposal inlet. Also run the disposal to clear any food blocking the drain path.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter and hose are clear, listen for the drain pump running. If silent, test the pump motor with a multimeter. Replace if it shows no continuity.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump needs replacement or the clog is in the household plumbing.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher i20 error drain fix

faq.q: How do I drain the standing water manually?
faq.a: Use towels or a wet/dry vacuum to remove the standing water. You can also scoop it out with a cup and empty it into the sink.

faq.q: What is a garbage disposal knockout plug?
faq.a: New garbage disposals come with a plastic plug in the dishwasher inlet. If not removed during installation, it blocks the dishwasher from draining through the disposal.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Clean the dishwasher drain filter at least monthly, or more frequently if you do not pre-rinse dishes before loading.

title: Leak Detected - Anti-Flood Activated
shortDescription: The anti-flood system has detected water in the base of the dishwasher.
fullDescription: Error code i30 indicates the anti-flood protection system has been triggered. Water has accumulated in the base pan of the dishwasher, activating the float switch. The dishwasher will continuously run the drain pump to remove water and will not operate until the leak is resolved.

causes.cause: Loose or damaged hose clamp inside dishwasher
Overfilling due to faulty float switch

symptoms: Dishwasher will not start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Turn off and access base
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher at the breaker. Turn off the water supply. Pull the dishwasher out and tilt it back slightly to access the base. Place towels to catch any water.

fixSteps.title: Remove water from base
fixSteps.instruction: Soak up all water from the base pan with towels. The float switch must return to its down position to clear the error.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Run a short cycle with the kickplate removed to observe where water is leaking. Common sources: door seal, inlet valve connection, hose clamps, and spray arm seals.

fixSteps.title: Repair the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose clamps, replace damaged hoses, or replace the leaking component. Then dry the base completely and test.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source is a cracked tub or sump, or if you cannot identify where the leak is coming from.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher i30 error leak anti-flood fix

faq.q: Why does the pump run non-stop with i30?
faq.a: The anti-flood system activates the drain pump continuously to remove water from the base and prevent further damage.

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause i30?
faq.a: Excessive suds can cause water to overflow through seals into the base. Always use the recommended amount of dishwasher detergent.

faq.q: How do I reset i30?
faq.a: Remove all water from the base pan so the float switch drops. Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. When you turn it back on, the error should clear if the float is down.

title: Wash Motor or Diverter Valve Issue
shortDescription: The wash motor circuit or diverter valve has a fault.
fullDescription: Error code i40 indicates a problem with the wash motor (circulation pump) or the diverter valve that directs water to the upper and lower spray arms. The control board has detected an electrical fault in the circuit.

causes.cause: Blocked or jammed wash motor impeller
Wiring issue to motor or diverter

symptoms: Humming noise but no wash action

fixSteps.title: Check for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the spray arms and filter assembly. Check for glass, food debris, or other items that could jam the wash motor impeller.

fixSteps.title: Inspect diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the diverter valve (typically on top of the wash motor). Check that it moves freely and is not stuck or broken.

fixSteps.title: Test wash motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the wash motor underneath the dishwasher. Check the motor winding resistance with a multimeter. Also check for debris in the motor housing.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the wash motor or diverter valve as indicated by testing.

whenToCallPro: If the wash motor needs replacement, as it is located underneath the dishwasher and may require partial disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher i40 error wash motor fix

faq.q: What does the diverter valve do?
faq.a: The diverter valve alternates water flow between the upper and lower spray arms during the wash cycle to optimize cleaning.

faq.q: Can broken glass cause i40?
faq.a: Yes. Small glass fragments can jam the wash motor impeller, preventing it from spinning and triggering i40.

faq.q: Is the wash motor the same as the drain pump?
faq.a: No. The wash motor (circulation pump) sprays water through the spray arms. The drain pump removes water after the cycle. They are separate components.

title: Drain Motor Issue
shortDescription: The drain motor has a fault - either shorted or drawing excessive current.
fullDescription: Error code i50 indicates an electrical fault with the drain pump motor. The control board has detected the drain motor is drawing abnormal current, which could indicate a short circuit, seized motor, or wiring problem.

causes.cause: Foreign object jammed in drain pump
Short circuit in drain pump wiring
Control board drain pump driver failure

symptoms: Drain pump makes grinding or buzzing noise
Water does not drain or drains slowly

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter assembly and check the drain pump area for glass fragments, bones, seeds, or other debris that could jam the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Try to rotate the drain pump impeller by hand (accessible through the filter opening). It should spin freely.

fixSteps.title: Test drain pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump underneath the dishwasher. Disconnect the wiring and measure motor resistance with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is seized or tests faulty, replace the drain pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher i50 error drain pump fix

faq.q: Is i50 different from i20?
faq.a: Yes. i20 means the drain cycle took too long (usually a blockage). i50 specifically indicates an electrical problem with the drain pump motor itself.

faq.q: Can I hear if the drain pump is running?
faq.a: A working drain pump makes a consistent humming sound during the drain cycle. Grinding, buzzing, or silence indicates a problem.

faq.q: How long does a drain pump last?
faq.a: Typical dishwasher drain pumps last 7-12 years. Glass fragments and hard food debris can shorten the lifespan.

title: Heater or NTC Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water heating element or temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error code i60 indicates a fault in the water heating circuit or the NTC temperature sensor. The dishwasher cannot properly heat the wash water, which is necessary for effective cleaning and sanitizing.

causes.cause: Failed heater relay on control board

symptoms: Water does not heat during cycle
Dishes come out greasy or poorly cleaned

fixSteps.title: Test NTC temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor (usually near the sump or heater). Disconnect and measure resistance. At room temperature, expect 10K-50K ohms depending on model.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater leads. Measure resistance across the element terminals. Should read 10-30 ohms. Open or very low readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Check heater circuit wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all wiring connections in the heater circuit for corrosion, burns, or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the NTC sensor or heating element as indicated by testing.

whenToCallPro: If the heater relay on the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher i60 error heater NTC fix

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher without heat?
faq.a: The dishwasher will not complete cycles properly without heating. Cold water does not effectively dissolve detergent or sanitize dishes.

faq.q: Where is the heating element?
faq.a: In most Frigidaire dishwashers, the heating element is a circular element at the bottom of the tub, visible when the lower rack is removed.

faq.q: What temperature should dishwasher water reach?
faq.a: Dishwashers typically heat water to 120-150F (49-66C) during the wash cycle, with sanitize cycles reaching 155F (68C) or higher.

title: Electronic Control Board Fault
shortDescription: The main electronic control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: Error code iC0 indicates the main electronic control board has detected an internal malfunction. This could be a processor error, memory fault, or output driver failure. The dishwasher cannot operate safely and stops.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher at the circuit breaker for 10 minutes. A control board glitch may clear with a full power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door and inspect the control board area (usually behind the door panel) for moisture or condensation. Dry if found.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and reseat all wire harness connectors on the main control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, replace the main electronic control board.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement is straightforward but diagnosis requires ruling out other issues first.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher iC0 error control board fix

faq.q: Is the control board expensive?
faq.a: Frigidaire dishwasher control boards typically cost $80-$200 for the part, plus installation labor if using a professional.

faq.q: Can moisture damage the board permanently?
faq.a: Yes, if moisture causes corrosion or short circuits on the PCB. Drying may help temporarily, but corroded traces will eventually fail.

faq.q: Should I use a surge protector?
faq.a: Dishwashers are typically hardwired, making surge protection difficult. A whole-house surge protector at the electrical panel is the best protection.

title: Overfill Condition
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected a water overfill condition.
fullDescription: Error code iF0 indicates the dishwasher has filled with more water than expected. The overfill float switch has been triggered, indicating the water level is dangerously high. The dishwasher will attempt to drain and will not operate until the issue is resolved.

causes.cause: Control board not shutting off fill valve

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the water supply valve under the sink to stop water from entering the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Check float mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack and check the float (usually a small dome near the front of the tub). It should move up and down freely. Clear any debris preventing movement.

fixSteps.title: Test water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher off and water supply off, observe if water still trickles into the tub. A stuck-open valve will continue to fill even when powered off.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve is stuck open, replace it. Turn off the water supply, disconnect the inlet hose, remove the old valve, and install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is not shutting off the fill valve, the board or its relay may need professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher iF0 error overfill fix

faq.q: Is iF0 a flood risk?
faq.a: Yes. Turn off the water supply immediately. A stuck-open inlet valve will continue to fill the dishwasher beyond capacity.

faq.q: Where is the float?
faq.a: The overfill float is a small dome-shaped piece on the bottom front of the dishwasher tub. When water lifts it, it triggers the float switch underneath.

faq.q: Can I reset iF0 without fixing it?
faq.a: You can reset by cycling the breaker, but if the inlet valve is stuck open, the overfill will recur and potentially cause water damage.

title: Membrane Switch Error
shortDescription: The touchpad/membrane switch on the control panel has detected a stuck or shorted button.
fullDescription: Error code Er indicates the control board has detected that one or more buttons on the touchpad membrane switch are stuck in a pressed or shorted state. The dishwasher cannot accept user input until the touchpad issue is resolved.

causes.cause: Control board button input circuit failure

symptoms: Cannot select any cycles or options

fixSteps.title: Press each button
fixSteps.instruction: Press each button on the control panel firmly several times. A stuck button may release. Check for food or debris around the buttons.

fixSteps.title: Dry the touchpad area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe down the entire control panel with a dry cloth. Moisture from steam can seep under the touchpad and cause false button signals.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes. This clears any latched button states in the control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace touchpad assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the touchpad/membrane switch assembly needs replacement. This is part of the door panel on many models.

whenToCallPro: If the touchpad replacement does not fix the Er error, the control board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher Er error touchpad fix

faq.q: Why does steam cause touchpad issues?
faq.a: Hot steam from the wash cycle can condense under the touchpad membrane and create electrical shorts between button contacts.

faq.q: Can I replace just the touchpad?
faq.a: On some models the touchpad is separate from the control board. On others, they are an integrated unit. Check your model's parts breakdown.

faq.q: Is Er dangerous?
faq.a: Not dangerous, but a dishwasher that starts on its own (from stuck buttons) should be turned off at the breaker until repaired.

title: Vent Open/Wax Motor Failure
shortDescription: The vent wax motor has failed or the vent is stuck open.
fullDescription: Error code UO indicates the dishwasher's vent mechanism (controlled by a wax motor) is stuck open or the wax motor has failed. The vent is supposed to open during the drying cycle to release steam but should be closed during wash. If stuck open during wash, it will release steam into the kitchen.
symptoms: Steam escaping from top of door during wash
Moisture on counter or cabinets near dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Inspect vent area
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door. Locate the vent on the inside of the door (usually top area). Check if the vent flap moves freely.

fixSteps.title: Clean vent mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Clean around the vent flap for any debris or detergent buildup that may be preventing proper operation.

fixSteps.title: Test wax motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the wax motor behind the inner door panel. Disconnect and test with a multimeter. Apply 120V to verify it actuates.

fixSteps.title: Replace wax motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the two screws holding the wax motor. Disconnect the wire harness. Install the new motor and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable removing the inner door panel to access the wax motor.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher UO error vent wax motor fix

faq.q: What is a wax motor?
faq.a: A wax motor uses a wax element that expands when heated electrically. This expansion pushes a plunger that opens or closes the vent flap.

faq.q: Can steam damage my cabinets?
faq.a: Yes. If the vent is stuck open during the wash cycle, hot steam can damage wood cabinets, laminate, and countertops above the dishwasher.

faq.q: Do all dishwashers have vent wax motors?
faq.a: No. Only certain models with automatic vent systems use wax motors. Some models use a fan or a simpler passive vent.

title: Water Not Heating - Timeout
shortDescription: The water temperature did not reach the target within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error code HO indicates the dishwasher's water heater was unable to raise the water temperature to the required level within the maximum heating time. The dishwasher may continue the cycle with cold water or stop.

causes.cause: Heater relay failure on control board

symptoms: Cycle runs much longer than normal
Dishes not clean, especially greasy items
Water feels cold when door is opened mid-cycle

fixSteps.title: Check hot water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Run the kitchen hot water faucet until it is hot before starting the dishwasher. This ensures hot water is immediately available at the inlet.

fixSteps.title: Verify water heater setting
fixSteps.instruction: Check your household water heater thermostat. It should be set to at least 120F (49C) for proper dishwasher operation.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the heating element. It should read 10-30 ohms. Open or very high readings mean the element is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Test temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Check the NTC sensor resistance at room temperature. Compare to specification. Replace if out of range.

whenToCallPro: If the heating element and sensor both test good, the control board heater circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher HO error water not heating fix

faq.q: Should I run hot water before starting the dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes. Running the kitchen faucet hot first purges cold water from the pipes, so the dishwasher fills with hot water from the start.

faq.q: Can cold water damage the dishwasher?
faq.a: Cold water will not damage the dishwasher, but it results in poor cleaning since detergent does not dissolve well in cold water.

faq.q: Is HO the same as i60?
faq.a: They are related. HO is a timeout warning that water did not heat in time. i60 indicates an actual fault with the heater or sensor hardware.

title: Low Rinse Aid Level
shortDescription: The rinse aid dispenser is low or empty.
fullDescription: Code LO indicates the rinse aid dispenser level is low. This is a maintenance reminder, not an error. The dishwasher will continue to operate normally but drying performance and spot-free results may decrease without rinse aid.

causes.cause: Rinse aid dispenser is empty or low
Rinse aid dispenser not closing properly
Using wrong type of rinse aid

symptoms: Water spots on glasses and silverware
Dishes not drying as well as usual

fixSteps.title: Refill rinse aid
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rinse aid dispenser cap on the inside of the door. Pour rinse aid (like Jet Dry) until the reservoir is full. Close the cap securely.

fixSteps.title: Adjust rinse aid dosage
fixSteps.instruction: Adjust the rinse aid dispenser setting. Higher settings release more rinse aid per cycle. Start at the middle setting and adjust based on results.

fixSteps.title: Check dispenser cap seal
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the rinse aid cap gasket for damage. A leaking cap can cause rinse aid to drain out quickly.

fixSteps.title: Test rinse aid sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the LO indicator stays on after filling, the sensor in the dispenser may be faulty. The sensor is typically a small float or reed switch.

whenToCallPro: Only if the sensor is faulty and the indicator stays on after refilling.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher LO rinse aid refill

faq.q: Do I really need rinse aid?
faq.a: Rinse aid significantly improves drying performance and reduces water spots. It is especially important with hard water.

faq.q: Can I use vinegar instead of rinse aid?
faq.a: While vinegar has some drying benefits, it is not recommended by manufacturers as it can damage rubber seals and gaskets over time.

faq.q: How often do I need to refill rinse aid?
faq.a: Typically every 1-2 months depending on usage and the dosage setting.

title: Power Failure Detected
shortDescription: A power interruption occurred during a dishwasher cycle.
fullDescription: Code PF indicates the dishwasher experienced a power failure during an active cycle. When power returns, the dishwasher displays PF to alert you that the cycle was interrupted. You need to restart the cycle.
symptoms: PF flashing on display after power returns

fixSteps.title: Restart the cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Press the Start/Cancel button to clear PF. Select your desired cycle and press Start to begin a new cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check circuit breaker
fixSteps.instruction: If there was no general power outage, check the dishwasher's circuit breaker in the electrical panel. Reset if tripped.

fixSteps.title: Check for loose connections
fixSteps.instruction: If PF occurs repeatedly without power outages, the electrical connections at the junction box under the dishwasher may be loose.

fixSteps.title: Have electrician inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If PF recurs, have an electrician check the circuit wiring, connections, and the dishwasher's power cord for intermittent faults.

whenToCallPro: If PF appears repeatedly without a known power outage, an electrician should inspect the wiring.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher PF power failure error fix

faq.q: Will PF go away on its own?
faq.a: PF stays on the display until you press a button. It is just a notification that power was lost during a cycle.

faq.q: Are my dishes clean after a PF?
faq.a: Probably not fully. The cycle was interrupted, so it is best to restart a complete cycle.

faq.q: Can PF damage the dishwasher?
faq.a: Occasional PF codes from power outages will not damage the dishwasher. However, frequent power interruptions can stress the control board.

title: Door Not Fully Closed
shortDescription: The door is not fully latched, preventing the cycle from starting.
fullDescription: Code CL indicates the dishwasher door is not fully closed and latched. The door latch switch must confirm the door is secure before the dishwasher will begin any cycle. This is a safety interlock.
symptoms: Door appears closed but not latching
Clicking sound when trying to latch

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door fully and close it again with a firm push. Make sure the latch clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and check that no dishes, utensils, or racks are preventing the door from closing fully.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door latch alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the door latch aligns with the strike plate on the tub. Adjust the strike plate position if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test door latch switch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the door latch switch. Test continuity with the door latched (should be closed) and unlatched (should be open). Replace if faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the latch mechanism is broken or the switch needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher CL door not closed error fix

faq.q: Why does the door not latch?
faq.a: The most common reason is a dish or tall item in the upper rack blocking the door. Also check that the dishwasher is level and properly installed.

faq.q: Can a warped door cause CL?
faq.a: Yes, though rare. A door that has been bumped hard or a dishwasher that is not level can cause alignment issues with the latch.

faq.q: Is CL a real error?
faq.a: CL is more of a status indicator than a fault code. It simply means the door needs to be closed before the cycle can start.

title: Hall Sensor Error - Wash Motor
shortDescription: The wash motor hall sensor is not providing valid feedback signals.
fullDescription: Error code HS indicates the hall sensor on the wash motor (circulation pump) is not functioning correctly. The hall sensor monitors the motor speed and position, and without this feedback the control board cannot properly operate the wash motor.

causes.cause: Defective hall sensor in wash motor

symptoms: Wash motor does not run or runs intermittently

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes. A temporary sensor glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Access the wash motor under the dishwasher. Check the wiring harness connection to the motor for looseness or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Test hall sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The hall sensor is typically integrated into the wash motor. Test for proper voltage signals during operation using an oscilloscope or by checking resistance at the sensor connector.

fixSteps.title: Replace wash motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the hall sensor is integrated and faulty, the entire wash motor assembly must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: Hall sensor diagnosis and wash motor replacement typically require professional skills.
videoSearchQuery: Frigidaire dishwasher HS error hall sensor wash motor fix

faq.q: What is a hall sensor?
faq.a: A hall sensor is a small electronic device that uses magnetic fields to detect the position and speed of the motor rotor. It provides feedback to the control board for precise motor control.

faq.q: Can I replace just the hall sensor?
faq.a: In most dishwashers, the hall sensor is integrated into the wash motor and cannot be replaced separately. The entire motor assembly must be replaced.

faq.q: Is HS a common error?
faq.a: HS is not as common as drain or fill errors. It typically indicates a motor hardware failure that develops over time.
